papers.title: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan versus Trastuzumab Emtansine for HER2-Positive Breast Cancer (DESTINY-Breast03)
papers.abstract: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) demonstrated superior progression-free survival compared with trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) in patients with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer previously treated with trastuzumab and a taxane. Median PFS was 28.8 months vs. 6.8 months.

papers.title: T-DXd in HER2-Low Breast Cancer: DESTINY-Breast04 Results
papers.abstract: Trastuzumab deruxtecan significantly improved progression-free and overall survival versus physician's choice chemotherapy in patients with HER2-low metastatic breast cancer. This trial redefines HER2-low as a distinct targetable population beyond HER2-positive disease.

papers.title: Tucatinib combined with trastuzumab and capecitabine for HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer (HER2CLIMB-02)
papers.abstract: The HER2CLIMB-02 trial demonstrated that adding tucatinib to T-DM1 improved progression-free survival in HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer. The combination showed particular benefit in patients with brain metastases, a challenging population with limited treatment options.

papers.title: Mechanisms of resistance to HER2-targeted therapies in breast cancer
papers.abstract: Primary and acquired resistance to HER2-targeted agents involves HER2 mutation, PI3K/AKT pathway activation, RAS-MAPK signaling, and altered receptor recycling. Combination strategies targeting bypass pathways represent the next therapeutic frontier in HER2-positive disease.

papers.title: Neratinib for HER2-mutant non-breast cancers: pan-tumor analysis
papers.abstract: HER2-activating mutations occur across multiple tumor types including lung, colorectal, biliary, and bladder cancers. Neratinib demonstrated activity across histologies, supporting HER2 mutation as a tissue-agnostic biomarker for targeted therapy selection.

papers.title: DESTINY-Breast05: T-DXd versus T-DM1 in residual HER2-positive early breast cancer
papers.abstract: DESTINY-Breast05 investigates T-DXd versus T-DM1 in patients with HER2-positive early breast cancer with residual disease after neoadjuvant therapy. Interim data show favorable pathological complete response improvement with T-DXd in high-risk populations.

papers.title: HER2 heterogeneity and clonal evolution under targeted therapy pressure
papers.abstract: Intra-tumor HER2 heterogeneity contributes to therapeutic escape under anti-HER2 pressure. Single-cell sequencing reveals clonal dynamics during HER2-targeted therapy, with non-HER2-amplified clones expanding during treatment and driving acquired resistance.

papers.title: Antibody-drug conjugate payloads and bystander effects in HER2-positive tumors
papers.abstract: T-DXd's membrane-permeable DXd payload mediates potent bystander killing of adjacent HER2-negative tumor cells, explaining its activity in HER2-heterogeneous and HER2-low tumors. Linker stability and drug-to-antibody ratio optimization remain active areas of ADC development.

papers.title: Interstitial lung disease with trastuzumab deruxtecan: incidence, management, and risk factors
papers.abstract: ILD/pneumonitis occurred in 12.1% of T-DXd-treated patients, with 1.9% grade 5 events. Risk factors include prior ILD, high-dose corticosteroid use, and smoking history. Standardized monitoring protocols and early intervention reduce severity and treatment discontinuation.

papers.title: ctDNA dynamics as a predictive biomarker for HER2-targeted therapy response
papers.abstract: Early ctDNA clearance during anti-HER2 therapy predicts pathological complete response and long-term survival. Liquid biopsy monitoring of HER2 amplification and co-occurring mutations provides real-time pharmacodynamic readouts superior to imaging alone.

papers.title: Pyrotinib plus capecitabine versus lapatinib plus capecitabine for HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer (PHOEBE trial)
papers.abstract: Pyrotinib demonstrated superior progression-free survival compared with lapatinib in HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer, establishing it as an effective second-line TKI option in Asian patient populations. The trial highlights geographic variation in HER2-targeted therapy outcomes.

papers.title: DESTINY-Breast06: T-DXd in HER2-ultralow and HER2-low breast cancer
papers.abstract: T-DXd significantly improved progression-free survival versus chemotherapy in HER2-low and HER2-ultralow hormone receptor-positive metastatic breast cancer. These data further expand the targetable HER2 population and challenge traditional HER2 testing classification cutoffs.
